guidance.sourceOfTruth: For every comparison, retrieve both fighter objects and compare totalScore numerically. The higher totalScore is the verdict winner.
guidance.scope: Judge UFC accomplishments by default. Non-UFC achievements may be mentioned only as context.
guidance.verdictRule: Use verdictWinner exactly when a matchup object exists. headToHead.seriesWinner names only the real fight or series winner and never overrides verdictWinner.
guidance.retrievalRule: Do not issue a verdict until both fighter records have been found. If either fighter is missing, say the data is incomplete.
explainWith: title-fight wins and adjusted title credit
writingStyle: Separate better fighter from better UFC resume when relevant
avoid: raw formula narration in normal answers
using a head-to-head winner as the comparison winner when totalScore says otherwise

headToHead.summary: Pereira stopped Adesanya to win the middleweight title, then Adesanya knocked Pereira out in the rematch to regain it. In UFC terms, the rivalry is split.
Esparza beat Grasso before Grasso’s flyweight title rise.

headToHead.seriesWinner: Valentina Shevchenko 1-1-1 edge by final title result
headToHead.summary: Grasso submitted Valentina to win the belt, retained in a draw, then lost the trilogy. This rivalry defines Grasso’s ceiling and Valentina’s later-career context.
Topuria knocked out Volkanovski to win the featherweight title, giving Ilia a direct era-shifting win over one of the division's greatest champions.
Islam beat Volkanovski twice at lightweight. Volk gets upward-division context, but Islam still owns the direct championship separation.
Volkanovski beat Aldo before winning the belt, giving Volk a clean bridge win between featherweight eras.
Volkanovski beat Holloway three times, took the featherweight belt from him, and owned the clearest separation of their shared era.
Sterling defended the bantamweight title against Cejudo by split decision. Cejudo has two-division champion value, but Sterling owns the direct UFC result.
Sterling went 2-0 against Yan officially. The DQ title win needs context, but the rematch decision gives Sterling the direct UFC series edge.
Sterling stopped Dillashaw in a bantamweight title fight. The win counts, but Dillashaw’s shoulder injury keeps it from being treated like a clean prime champion result.
Nunes knocked out Cyborg in 51 seconds, the defining direct separation in their UFC résumés.
Peña shocked Nunes to win the belt, then Nunes dominated the rematch. This rivalry defines Peña’s ceiling.
Nunes submitted Tate at UFC 200 to take the bantamweight title, ending Tate’s prime championship window.
Nunes finished Rousey quickly in a UFC bantamweight title fight. It is a clean direct result and a symbolic passing-of-the-torch moment in women’s UFC history.
Nunes beat Shevchenko twice, including a close title fight. Valentina's case stays elite, but Amanda owns the direct edge in the women's GOAT debate.
Chael nearly pulled off the upset in the first fight, but Anderson finished him twice. This rivalry defines Chael’s peak and ceiling.
Anderson submitted Henderson in a UFC middleweight title fight. Hendo brought elite credentials, but Anderson owns the direct UFC championship result.
Bisping beat Anderson in a five-round fight, one of the biggest name wins of his UFC résumé.

Edgar went 3-0 against Penn, including the lightweight title win, the clearer rematch defense, and a later featherweight finish. Penn has the broader all-time aura, but Edgar owns the direct UFC series.
GSP beat Penn twice, including a dominant championship rematch, which creates clear separation between them in the all-time debate.
Penn beat Hughes twice, including the welterweight title upset, while Hughes won the middle fight. The series is a huge part of Penn's two-division argument.
Henderson won both UFC title fights; the rematch remains debated.
Cain stopped Brock to win the heavyweight title, exposing the ceiling of Brock’s short-window reign.
Brock beat Couture to win the UFC heavyweight title, the launch point of his short but real championship case.
Werdum submitted Cain to unify the heavyweight title.
Esparza beat Rose twice in UFC title fights: first for the inaugural belt, then to regain the title years later.
Zhang submitted Esparza to take the title, ending Esparza’s second reign.
Jones stopped Chael in a light heavyweight title fight; this is upward-division title-challenge context for Chael.
Chael edged Bisping in a key middleweight contender fight, one of Chael’s best wins and a useful Bisping loss-context point.
Rashad stopped Chael late in Chael’s UFC run, treated as a post-prime/exit result.
Oliveira submitted Poirier in a UFC lightweight title fight. Poirier has deeper name volume, but Oliveira owns the direct undisputed-title finish.
Edgar beat Oliveira before Oliveira’s lightweight title peak. It is useful quality-win context for Edgar, but not treated like beating champion Oliveira.
Islam submitted Oliveira to win the lightweight title, giving him a defining championship win over an elite modern lightweight.
Oliveira submitted Gaethje in a chaotic lightweight title-level fight. Gaethje adds danger and schedule strength, but Oliveira owns the direct finish.
Oliveira dominated post-prime Tony, a major Charles proof point but not peak-Tony scoring.
Couture beat Chuck first, but Liddell won the next two by knockout and took the rivalry separation during the light heavyweight title era.
Evans scored an iconic knockout.
Liddell beat Ortiz twice in major light heavyweight rivalry fights, including a title defense that helped define Chuck's championship era.
Poirier went 2-1 against McGregor in UFC fights. McGregor won the early featherweight meeting, but Poirier took the lightweight rematch and trilogy result.
Conor knocked out Aldo in 13 seconds, one of the biggest legacy moments in UFC history. Aldo still has the larger long-term featherweight legacy, but that result is impossible to ignore.
Khabib submitted Conor in their lightweight title fight, which gives Khabib clear separation in one of the most important fights of the modern era.
Conor beat a young Holloway in 2013. That matters historically, but Max later built a much deeper long-term resume, so that fight does not automatically decide the all-time ranking.
Cyborg beat Holm over five rounds in her strongest UFC title defense.
Cormier dominated and submitted Henderson in a UFC light heavyweight contender fight. Hendo’s legend status remains, but DC owns the direct UFC result.
Henderson knocked Bisping out at UFC 100, then lost the later UFC middleweight title rematch. It is one of the cleanest split-series résumé debates.
Belfort won the UFC series 2-1.
Jones beat Cormier officially in their first fight, while the rematch was overturned to a no contest. Even with the messy context, Jones holds the direct rivalry separation.
Cormier knocked out Stipe first, but Stipe won the next two and took the heavyweight trilogy. That gives Stipe the direct rivalry edge.
Cruz beat DJ at bantamweight before DJ became the flyweight standard. It matters historically, but it should be treated with division and career-stage context.
DJ finished Cejudo in their first fight, then Cejudo edged the rematch to win the flyweight title. The split series keeps the debate nuanced instead of one-sided.
Cejudo finished Cruz in Cruz's comeback-era bantamweight title challenge. It helps Cejudo's compact legacy, but Cruz's broader case still needs injury and layoff context.
Cruz beat Dillashaw by split decision to reclaim the bantamweight title. It is a close direct result, but Cruz owns the official title-fight win.
Islam submitted Poirier in a UFC lightweight title fight. Poirier showed veteran danger, but Islam owns the direct title result.
Poirier and Gaethje split two violent UFC fights: Poirier finished the first meeting, Gaethje won the BMF rematch by head-kick knockout. The series is a true lightweight résumé debate.
Khabib submitted Poirier in a lightweight title defense, one of the key wins in Khabib's perfect compact reign.
Khabib submitted Poirier in an undisputed lightweight title fight. Poirier has a great résumé, but Khabib owns the direct championship result.
Poirier beat Holloway twice, including their interim lightweight title fight. It matters, but Max's featherweight legacy still carries separate all-time weight.

Poirier leads the UFC trilogy 2-1, including the 2019 interim lightweight title win. Max owns the final BMF/retirement-fight result and still has the stronger UFC-only GOAT case because of featherweight championship dominance.
Miocic knocked out Werdum to win the title.
Each won one UFC fight.
Evans stopped Griffin to win the title.
Griffin won the UFC trilogy 2-1.
Stipe controlled Ngannou in their first title fight, then Ngannou knocked him out in the rematch. The split keeps both heavyweight cases connected.
Shamrock stopped Ortiz in his defining defense.
Aldo beat Edgar twice in UFC featherweight title-level fights, which is a major part of Aldo's UFC resume.
Aldo beat Edgar twice in featherweight title-level fights. Edgar’s second-act featherweight résumé is strong, but Aldo owns the direct championship results.
Holloway beat Edgar in a UFC featherweight title fight. Edgar brings longevity and title history, but Max owns the direct result.
Hughes beat young GSP first, but GSP won the next two and took clear generational separation at welterweight.
GSP submitted Bisping to win the middleweight title. In this model, it counts as a prime title loss for Bisping because he was defending champion.
Jones won their title fight.
Glover knocked out Evans.
Merab beat Cejudo, adding a two-division champion to his modern bantamweight resume.
Cejudo stopped Dillashaw in a flyweight title fight. Dillashaw was moving down, but Cejudo owns the direct champion-vs-champion result.
Kayla submitted Holm in her UFC debut; post-prime Holm context but strong Kayla proof.
Tate submitted Holm late to take the belt, capping Holm’s title reign at zero defenses.
Holm knocked out Ronda to win the UFC bantamweight title, one of the biggest upset wins ever.
Gaethje beat Topuria to win the undisputed UFC lightweight title. This is the title win that turns his case from action résumé into real champion résumé.
Topuria beat Holloway in a major featherweight legacy fight. Max still owns the longer all-time resume, but Ilia owns the direct result.
Adesanya beat Whittaker twice, first to unify the middleweight title and later to defend it. Those wins are central to Izzy's title-era case.
Andrade finished Dern in a key contender fight, a strong late-prime Andrade win and Dern loss-context hit.
Andrade knocked out Rose to win the title; Rose won the rematch. This is Andrade’s best title proof and Rose volatility context.
Zhang quickly stopped Andrade to take the strawweight title, capping Andrade’s reign.
Rose went 2-0 against Joanna in UFC strawweight title fights: one title-winning finish and one five-round title defense.
Valentina beat Joanna to win the UFC flyweight title. Joanna still owns the stronger strawweight-specific case, but Valentina owns the direct UFC championship result.
Zhang went 2-0 against Joanna in UFC fights: one all-time classic title defense and one violent rematch finish. It is direct strawweight separation.
Holloway finished Aldo twice in featherweight title fights, which gives Max clear separation even though Aldo's broader historical legacy remains massive.
Merab beat Aldo in a key bantamweight contender fight, giving him a direct win over a featherweight legend during Aldo's late elite run.
Kayla submitted Peña to win the title, ending Peña’s second reign.
Valentina submitted Peña, a key elite-loss context point for Peña.
Khabib submitted Gaethje in his final UFC fight, closing his prime with one of the cleanest title-run finishes ever.
Khabib submitted Gaethje in a UFC lightweight title fight. Gaethje had real danger, but Khabib owns the clean direct championship result.
Holloway knocked out Gaethje in one of the signature UFC moments. Gaethje still has a strong lightweight résumé, but Max owns the direct result.
Gaethje stopped Tony in the interim/title-level fight that ended Tony’s prime title case.
Machida won the first fight; Shogun won the rematch.
Hughes dominated Royce in the 2006 return fight. It matters as direct matchup history but is post-prime in Royce’s UFC GOAT scoring.

Merab beat Yan over five rounds in 2023 with pace, wrestling pressure, and control. Yan then beat Merab by unanimous decision at UFC 323 to reclaim the bantamweight title, making the rivalry a split series instead of a one-way direct edge.
Merab beat O'Malley twice in title fights, giving him direct championship separation over one of the era's biggest bantamweight stars.
Rousey submitted Tate in their UFC title fight. The broader Strikeforce rivalry is historically important, but only the UFC fight counts in this ranking.
Couture won the UFC trilogy 2-1.
Woodley knocked out Lawler to win the welterweight title and end Robbie’s reign.
Rose beat Zhang twice in UFC strawweight title fights, including one finish. This is Rose’s strongest head-to-head GOAT argument and Zhang’s clearest ceiling.
Valentina beat Zhang in an upward-division flyweight title challenge. It matters, but carries reduced loss-context damage compared with a same-division title loss.
